statute: Oregon Revised Statutes § 701.005 et seq. (Oregon Construction Contractors Board)
license_label: Oregon Construction Contractors Board (CCB) License Number
license_notes: Oregon requires ALL construction contractors to hold a CCB license, including handyman services and home inspectors. No lien rights for unlicensed contractors. 16-hour RMI training required before licensing.

mandatory_clauses.label: Oregon CCB license disclosure
3-day right to cancel (home solicitation)
mandatory_clauses.text:
RIGHT TO CANCEL: If this contract was solicited at your home, you have three business days to cancel without penalty. Send written notice to:
{{contractor_name}}, {{contractor_address}}

mandatory_clauses.label: Bond and insurance notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
Bond: Oregon law requires this contractor to maintain a bond through the Oregon Construction Contractors Board.
General Liability Insurance: {{gl_status}} — Provider: {{gl_provider}}

mandatory_clauses.label: Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: 1st offense $1,000 fine; property owner complaint = $5,000. Repeat offenses = $5,000. Just bidding without license = $500 fine. No lien rights for unlicensed contractors.
notes: Oregon requires ALL contractors to have a CCB license. Includes residential, commercial, residential+commercial combinations. Endorsements for specialty trades. 16-hour RMI law and business training required. Bond amounts vary by license type. Inspectors regularly visit job sites to verify licensing. No lien rights for unlicensed contractors. Portland area has additional Metro registration and income tax requirements.
